episodes.title: Melania Trump distances herself from deceased sex-offender Jeffrey Epstein
episodes.description:
US First Lady Melania Trump has sought to create distance between herself and the deceased sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, in a statement that took most observers by surprise.

Mrs Trump said she had never been friends with Epstein and had no knowledge of the abuses he was perpetrating. 

She also called on Congress to give Epstein's victims a public hearing and obtain their testimony under oath. 

For more news, visit sabcnews.com and #SABCNews on all Social Media platforms.

episodes.title: I never had a relationship with Jeffery Epstein: US First Lady Melania Trump
episodes.description:
US First Lady Melania Trump has sought to create distance between herself and the deceased sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, in a statement that took most observers by surprise.

episodes.title: Religious cults in SA: Thoko Mkhwanazi-Xaluva, Pontsho Pilane weigh in
episodes.description:
As South Africa continues to grapple with the rise of religious movements operating outside mainstream structures, questions are intensifying around where faith ends and control begins.  In KwaMaphumulo, north of Durban, a growing congregation known as Ekhaya Labafundi bakaJehova has drawn more than 50 followers who have cut ties with their families, abandoned careers, and in some cases medical treatment in pursuit of a life devoted entirely to their beliefs. For more on this we are joined by the Chairperson of the South African Commission for the Promotion and Protection of Cultural, Religious and Linguistic Communities, Thoko Mkhwanazi-Xaluva and  Author and Journalist Pontsho Pilane.

For more news, visit sabcnews.com and #SABCNews on all Social Media platforms.

episodes.title: Secluded religious community in KwaMaphumulo - cut off from the world - redefining 'devotion to God'
episodes.description:
A secluded religious community is redefining devotion in KwaMaphumulo, north of Durban.

More than 50 followers have cut ties with the outside world, leaving behind families, careers and conventional life.

Congregants from across the country now live on the property full-time, calling themselves an independent kingdom.

Members of Ekhaya Labafundi do not socialise beyond their church.

They also reject clinics and hospitals, saying medical care goes against their faith.

For more news, visit sabcnews.com and #SABCNews on all Social Media platforms.

episodes.title: Epstein files continue to reveal more
episodes.description:
A group of United Nations Independent Human Rights experts says the Epstein Files contain disturbing and credible evidence of systematic and large-scale sexual abuse, trafficking and exploitation of women and girls that could amount to crimes against humanity. The 9 experts, who serve as Special Rapportuers appointed by the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva, warned that these acts could amount to sexual slavery, reproductive violence, enforced disappearance, torture, inhuman and degrading treatment and femicide. The group argued further that despite the disclosures in the reams of documents released by the US Justice Department, accountability had been limited. Sherwin Bryce-Pease reports.

For more news, visit sabcnews.com and #SABCNews on all Social Media platforms.

episodes.title: SA Epstein survivor details alleged abuse
episodes.description:
South African Julliette Bryant has opened about about her very first encounter with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein the in mid-2000's. 

She has detailed how she met him in Cape Town, along with former US president Bill Clinton. 

Bryant talks to us now about her three weeks encounter, she was in America, beginning her traumatic experiences with Epstein.

For more news, visit sabcnews.com and #SABCNews on all Social Media platforms.

episodes.title: Zimbabwean police arrest leader of suspected cult Ishmael Chokurongerwa
episodes.description:
Zimbabwean police have arrested leader of suspected cult Ishmael Chokurongerwa on allegations of child abuse and forced marriages.

Ishmael was arrested at one of his farm on the outskirts of the capital Harare along with four other leaders

For more news, visit sabcnews.com and #SABCNews on all Social Media platforms.

episodes.title: SA has a steady growth of religious cult groups: Prof Farid Esack
episodes.description:
South Africa has witnessed a steady growth of religious cult groups.
Professor Farid Esack from the University of Johannesburg says Jo'burg has the most cults, followed by Durban and then to a lesser extent Cape Town. For more insight on how these cults operate we now have Prof Esack joining us on the line from Johannesburg.

For more news, visit: sabcnews.com
